title: Motor Oil on Alcantara / Microsuede
stainChemistry: Classification: Oil/Grease (petroleum hydrocarbon). Motor oil is a complex mixture of petroleum hydrocarbons (C15-C50 alkanes, cycloalkanes) with additives. Alcantara is a polyester-polyurethane microfiber composite — petroleum solvents dissolve the polyurethane binder, and alcohol strips the fiber coating. Only water-based cleaners are safe. Oil sits on the tight microfiber surface initially but penetrates over time.
whyThisWorks: Alcantara's tight microfiber weave means oil sits on the surface longer than on natural fibers, giving a window for absorption and water-based treatment. Cornstarch pulls oil out without chemicals, and water-based cleaners emulsify residual oil safely without attacking the polyurethane binder that holds the fibers together.
defaultAssumption: Treat as unknown age / possibly dried.

steps: 1. Blot excess motor oil immediately with clean paper towels. Do not rub — blot gently from outside in to prevent spreading. Replace towels as they absorb oil.

2. Apply cornstarch or talcum powder generously over the stain. Press gently into the surface. Let sit 15–20 minutes to absorb oil from the microfiber. Brush off with a soft brush. Repeat if powder darkens significantly.

3. Apply water-based upholstery cleaner or water-based degreaser to the stain. Work gently with a soft-bristle brush, brushing with the grain of the Alcantara. Avoid all solvents — they dissolve the polyurethane binder. Water-based cleaners only.
4. Blot with a clean cloth dampened with cool water to remove cleaner residue. Do not saturate — use minimal moisture. Blot dry with a clean towel.
5. Air dry away from direct heat and sunlight. Once dry, brush gently with a soft suede brush to restore the nap and texture of the Alcantara.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool — no heat; heat damages polyurethane binder in Alcantara
warnings: Never use acetone, petroleum solvents, or alcohol — dissolves polyurethane binder
Never use steam or heat — damages microfiber structure
Never rub aggressively — disrupts nap and causes permanent pilling
Always brush with the grain, never against it

steps: 1. Blot excess oil immediately with paper towels. Do not rub.
2. Cover the stain generously with cornstarch or baking soda. Let sit at least 20 minutes. The powder absorbs oil from the microfiber.
3. Brush off powder with a soft brush, brushing with the grain.
4. Apply a small amount of water-based dish soap (like Dawn) diluted in cool water to a soft cloth. Gently dab the stain — do not scrub or rub.
5. Blot clean with a cloth dampened with cool water. Blot dry.
6. Air dry completely. Brush gently with a soft suede brush to restore the nap.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: If the stain persists after two treatment attempts, take the item to a professional auto detailer who specializes in Alcantara care.
warnings: Never use acetone, petroleum solvents, or rubbing alcohol on Alcantara.
Always use water-based cleaners only — solvents destroy the material.
Never apply heat to speed drying.
Always brush with the grain to maintain texture.

neverDo: Use acetone — dissolves polyurethane binder permanently
Use petroleum solvents — dissolves polyurethane binder
Use alcohol or rubbing alcohol — strips fiber coating
Use steam or heat — damages microfiber structure
Rub or scrub aggressively — causes permanent pilling and nap damage
fiberSensitivities: Alcantara/Microsuede: NEVER use acetone, petroleum solvents, alcohol, or steam. Water-based cleaners ONLY. Always brush with the grain. Condition is not needed (unlike leather).

escalation.when: Oil has penetrated deeply into the microfiber, stain remains after absorbent + water-based treatment, or material texture has been damaged
escalation.whatToTell: Motor oil stain on Alcantara. Treated with absorbent powder and water-based cleaner. Oil may have penetrated the microfiber weave. Needs professional auto detailing.
escalation.specialistType: auto detailer specializing in Alcantara/suede care

contextQuestions: Is the Alcantara in a car (steering wheel, seats, headliner)?
How old is the motor oil stain?
Has any solvent or cleaner already been applied?
Is the Alcantara color light or dark?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Steering wheel Alcantara is thinner and more vulnerable than seat Alcantara. If a solvent has already been applied, the polyurethane binder may be compromised — professional assessment needed. Light Alcantara shows oil staining more and may need professional re-dyeing.
